nodes.title: Requirements Grouping
nodes.summary: Introduces how the standard’s requirements are grouped and framed, including the main safety disciplines covered. It provides a conceptual map for navigating safety management, safety engineering, and in-service safety requirements.

nodes.title: Requirements for safety achievement, assurance, and management
nodes.summary: Sets out overarching expectations for achieving, assuring, and managing safety across the contracted scope. It anchors the standard’s intent and links to related Defence Standard requirements.

nodes.title: Scope and Applicability
nodes.summary: Defines the applicability of the standard and the responsibilities for safety between the contractor and UK MOD. It establishes the life-cycle perspective and the baseline context for safety case and health and safety considerations.

nodes.title: Purpose and Scope
nodes.summary: States the purpose of the standard and the boundaries of what it covers for safety of deliverable PSS. It clarifies that requirements address achieving, assuring, and managing safety within the contract scope.

nodes.title: Application of Standard
nodes.summary: Explains how the standard is to be applied in contracts, including how accountability and safety management system expectations are interpreted. It provides the practical entry point for tailoring and applying requirements to a specific PSS contract.

nodes.title: Accountability and Responsibility
nodes.summary: Specifies how accountability and responsibility for safety are to be defined and managed between parties. It supports consistent assignment of safety duties across the contract and lifecycle.

nodes.title: Safety Management System
nodes.summary: Introduces expectations for the contractor’s safety management system as used to meet the standard’s requirements. It links the standard’s clauses to an organisational system for planning, control, and assurance.

nodes.title: Technical Information
nodes.summary: Provides the reference framework for the standard, including normative and informative sources used to interpret and apply requirements. It supports traceability to external standards and guidance.

nodes.title: Normative References
nodes.summary: Lists the normative documents that are required for correct application of the standard. These references define mandatory external requirements and definitions that the standard relies upon.

nodes.title: Informative References
nodes.summary: Lists supporting (non-mandatory) references that provide additional guidance for implementing the standard. It connects the standard to related MOD systems and widely used safety and engineering standards.

nodes.title: Definitions
nodes.summary: Defines key terms and how mandatory requirements and notes are to be interpreted within the standard. It provides the vocabulary and interpretation rules needed for consistent application and compliance assessment.

nodes.title: Mandatory Requirements
nodes.summary: Explains how mandatory requirements are expressed and should be interpreted (e.g., use of “shall”). It supports consistent compliance evaluation and links to authoritative glossaries/process references where relevant.

nodes.title: Safety Management System
nodes.summary: Specifies the safety management system expectations, including planning, agreement, review/update, and progress reporting. It links the contractor’s safety management plan and governance activities to recognised MOD and regulatory guidance.

nodes.title: Safety Management Plan
nodes.summary: Defines the role and expected content of the Safety Management Plan (SMP) as the primary planning and control artefact for safety activities. It positions the SMP within MOD guidance and related safety management frameworks.

nodes.title: Legislation, Regulations, Standards, Policy and Approved Codes of Practice
nodes.summary: Requires identification, documentation, and agreement of applicable legal and policy obligations and relevant standards/codes of practice. It ensures the safety approach is aligned with external regulatory and MOD policy expectations.

nodes.title: Information Management
nodes.summary: Defines how safety information is to be managed, including visibility of safety activities, definition and maintenance of the information set, and configuration/consistency controls. It establishes expectations for preserving, accessing, and securing safety-relevant information over time.

nodes.title: Contractors shall ensure that competent personnel are appointed
nodes.summary: Requires appointment of competent personnel for safety-relevant roles and points to recognised competence frameworks and guidance. It supports assurance that safety tasks are performed by suitably qualified practitioners.

nodes.title: Safety Audits
nodes.summary: Defines the audit regime for safety, including audit planning, reporting, independence, and remedial action. It establishes assurance mechanisms to verify that safety management and engineering activities are effective and compliant.

nodes.title: Contractor Safety Auditor Independence
nodes.summary: Specifies independence requirements for the Contractor Safety Auditor (CSA) to ensure objective assurance. It links to external codes of practice for independent safety assessors/auditors.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements, Hazard and Risk Analysis
nodes.summary: Covers the core safety engineering workflow: hazard identification, hazard tracking, safety requirements definition/management, safety analysis, and risk estimation/evaluation. It provides the main technical requirements for demonstrating and managing safety risk across the PSS lifecycle.

nodes.title: Hazards and Accidents
nodes.summary: Specifies requirements for identifying hazards and potential accidents and applying systematic analysis processes, including consideration of human factors and cyber security. It sets the foundation for subsequent hazard tracking and risk management activities.

nodes.title: Hazard Tracking
nodes.summary: Defines how hazards are to be tracked and controlled, including maintaining a hazard log and producing hazard log reports. It ensures visibility of hazard status and control effectiveness throughout the contract.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements
nodes.summary: Specifies how safety requirements are identified, documented, and tracked, including derived safety requirements. It provides the linkage between hazard analysis outputs and formal requirements baselines.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements Management
nodes.summary: Defines management controls for safety requirements, including bi-directional traceability, validation, and evidence of fulfillment, and integration with the hazard log. It ensures requirements remain controlled, justified, and demonstrably satisfied.

nodes.title: Design for Safety
nodes.summary: Specifies how design activities must address safety requirements through mitigation strategy identification, implementation, and management. It connects safety requirements to design decisions and the demonstration of mitigation effectiveness.

nodes.title: Safety Analysis
nodes.summary: Defines expectations for conducting, covering, using, and documenting safety analysis, and maintaining consistency with the evolving design. It supports evidence generation for safety case arguments and requirement satisfaction.

nodes.title: Failure Modes
nodes.summary: Requires identification and management of failure modes contributing to hazards, including visibility of control status and recording assumptions, data, and results. It ensures failure mode analysis supports hazard control and risk estimation across interfacing systems.

nodes.title: Risk Estimation
nodes.summary: Defines the process for estimating risk, including qualitative assessment, residual risk determination, and documentation. It supports consistent risk quantification/qualification and links to relevant standards where applicable.

nodes.title: Risk and Compliance Evaluation
nodes.summary: Specifies how estimated risks are evaluated against risk-to-life and other criteria, and how compliance is assessed and recorded. It provides the decision basis for acceptability and required mitigations or actions.

nodes.title: Satisfaction of Requirements
nodes.summary: Requires evidence-based demonstration that safety requirements (including derived requirements) have been met, and that methods can detect counter-evidence. It ties systems engineering activities and chosen verification/validation approaches back into the SMP.

nodes.title: Safety Reporting
nodes.summary: Defines required safety reporting outputs, including the Information Set Safety Summary (ISSS), Safety Case, and Safety Case Reports. It establishes how safety evidence and arguments are packaged and communicated to stakeholders for assurance and decision-making.

nodes.title: Information Set Safety Summary
nodes.summary: Specifies the ISSS as a structured summary drawn from the information set to provide visibility of safety engineering and analysis progress. It ensures integrators and operators receive sufficient information on assumptions, limitations, and scope.

nodes.title: Safety Case
nodes.summary: Defines the safety case as a structured argument supported by evidence from the information set, addressing safety across the required lifecycle scope. It includes expectations for compliance evidence, handling residual shortfalls, and maintaining/refining the safety case.

nodes.title: Safety Case Reports
nodes.summary: Specifies the content and purpose of safety case reports as deliverables that present key elements of the safety argument and evidence. It requires reporting of assumptions/limitations and rationale for operation where evidence shortfalls exist.

nodes.title: Supply and Change Management
nodes.summary: Defines how supply state and changes are controlled to preserve safety, including build state definition, change control, planning, and monitoring. It ensures safety impacts of changes are assessed and reflected in the information set and safety case.

nodes.title: Change Control
nodes.summary: Specifies the controls and governance for managing changes that may affect safety, including coordination with safety case considerations. It provides the mechanism to ensure changes are assessed, agreed, and tracked.

nodes.title: Supporting Systems In-Service
nodes.summary: Defines requirements for managing safety once systems are in service, including data management, monitoring/reporting, analysis, and remedial action. It ensures operational feedback informs the safety case and ongoing risk control.

nodes.title: Monitoring, Reporting and In-service Data Analysis
nodes.summary: Requires processes for collecting, analysing, and documenting safety-relevant in-service data, including incidents and near misses, and for updating the safety case accordingly. It also covers stakeholder notification, legislative monitoring, and FRACAS liaison where applicable.

nodes.title: Service Provision
nodes.summary: Specifies safety requirements for service provision, including safety case reporting for services, service planning, and risk management during operations. It ensures services are planned and controlled to manage risk to life and support accountable persons.

nodes.title: Risk Management
nodes.summary: Defines risk management expectations during service provision, including support to UK MOD for predicted or emergent risk to life and coordination with accountable persons for interfacing services. It introduces the ALARP concept as part of the risk decision framework.

nodes.title: Defence Standard 00-055
nodes.summary: External Defence Standard referenced for safety-related requirements, including programmable elements and related safety practices. It is used as a supporting or normative basis in multiple clauses of the document.

nodes.title: IEC 61508 Functional Safety of Electrical/Electronic/Programmable Electronic Safety-Related Systems
nodes.summary: International functional safety standard referenced as guidance for safety-related E/E/PE systems. It supports alignment of safety lifecycle and integrity practices with widely accepted functional safety principles.

nodes.title: Def Stan 05-057 Configuration Management of Defence Materiel
nodes.summary: Defence Standard referenced for configuration management practices relevant to controlling safety-related information and changes. It supports consistent configuration control across deliverables and lifecycle updates.

nodes.title: Def Stan 05-138 Cyber Security for Defence Suppliers
nodes.summary: Defence Standard referenced for cyber security expectations that intersect with safety, particularly in hazard analysis and risk estimation. It supports addressing cyber-related contributors to hazards and failures.

nodes.title: Def Stan 00-251 Human Factors Integration for Defence Systems
nodes.summary: Defence Standard referenced for integrating human factors into defence systems, supporting hazard analysis and safety engineering. It provides guidance for considering human contribution to hazards, failures, and mitigations.

nodes.title: STPA Handbook
nodes.summary: Reference source for System-Theoretic Process Analysis (STPA) used as a hazard analysis approach. It supports systematic identification of hazards and unsafe control actions beyond traditional failure-based methods.

nodes.title: IET Code of Practice for Independent Safety Assessors (COPISA)
nodes.summary: External code of practice referenced to support independence and good practice for independent safety assessment/audit roles. It provides a benchmark for auditor independence and professional conduct.

nodes.title: JSP 822
nodes.summary: Joint Service Publication referenced in the context of service risk management and safety governance. It supports alignment of service provision risk controls with MOD policy and processes.

nodes.title: Safety Case
nodes.summary: A structured safety argument supported by evidence demonstrating that a system is acceptably safe for a defined application and environment. It is a central concept used across reporting, change management, and assurance activities.

nodes.title: ALARP principle
nodes.summary: Defines the principle of reducing risk As Low As Reasonably Practicable as part of the risk decision framework. It is used to guide acceptability decisions and prioritisation of mitigations for risk to life.

nodes.title: Safety Management
nodes.summary: Covers the organisational planning, governance, and control activities used to manage safety across the contract and lifecycle. It frames how safety responsibilities, plans, and assurance activities are structured.

nodes.title: Safety Engineering
nodes.summary: Represents the technical activities used to identify hazards, derive and manage safety requirements, and design/verify mitigations. It underpins the evidence and argumentation used in safety reporting and safety cases.

nodes.title: Safety In-Service
nodes.summary: Covers safety management and assurance once systems are operational, including monitoring, reporting, data analysis, and remedial action. It ensures operational feedback informs ongoing risk control and safety case maintenance.
